_comment: Build-only jobs for workspace/run_lightrag.py. This file controls graph construction only; query_input_path and output_path are intentionally omitted here.

_knob_notes.max_parallel_insert: Document-group concurrency used by run_lightrag.py. Shares the global LLM semaphore with llm_model_max_async, so raising this alone does not increase LLM QPS.
_knob_notes.llm_model_max_async: Global LLM concurrency for the whole LightRAG instance (shared across all doc workers). Raising this is the only way to increase real LLM QPS.
_knob_notes.milvus.enable_sparse: If true, sparse vectors are stored during indexing so hybrid search can be enabled later.
_knob_notes.milvus.enable_hybrid_search: Build jobs may keep this true to validate hybrid-capable collections; query behavior is controlled by the query jobs file.
_knob_notes.logging.log_dir: Where runner logs and LightRAG logs are written. Relative paths resolve against the jobs file's directory, so 'logs' here means 'workspace/logs'.
_knob_notes.logging.name: File name prefix; the actual file is '<name>_<YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS>.log'.
_knob_notes.logging.level: LightRAG log level (DEBUG / INFO / WARNING / ERROR).
_knob_notes.working_dir_sharing: If two jobs point at the SAME working_dir, they merge into one knowledge graph: same doc_ids are skipped, new doc_ids are appended. Use distinct working_dirs when comparing chunking/embedding strategies.
